canto_title: The Prophecy of Exile and the Vision of the Great Lombard

scenes.summary: Dante compares his state of seeking truth to the myth of Climenè.
Beatrice commands Dante to express his desires clearly so that his needs may be understood.
Dante expresses his desire to understand the heavy prophecies he heard while descending into the underworld.
Beatrice responds to Dante's confession with clear, precise, and paternal words.
Beatrice explains how all earthly events are reflected in the eternal divine order.
Beatrice foretells Dante's necessary departure from Florence, comparing it to the fate of Hippolytus.
Beatrice describes the loss of loved ones, the difficulty of living on others' charity, and the bad company Dante will endure.
The prophecy identifies a great Lombard as Dante's first place of hospitality.

scenes.scene_name: The Rise of the Virtuous Leader
scenes.summary: Beatrice describes the emergence of a powerful, virtuous figure whose influence is already being felt.

scenes.scene_name: The Global Impact of the Leader
scenes.summary: The greatness of this leader's deeds will become known to all, changing the social order of rich and poor.
Beatrice warns Dante not to harbor envy toward his neighbors during his upcoming trials.
As Beatrice begins to weave her prophetic vision, Dante seeks guidance from her divine perspective.
Dante expresses his dread that his experiences may be too bitter for others to accept or that he will be forgotten by history.
The Light responds that although the truth may be harsh at first, it eventually provides vital nourishment.
Beatrice explains that these famous souls are shown to Dante to provide a clear and certain example.
